chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No.D-945 of 2020. [Syed Muzamil Abass Shah vs. Sindh Workers Welfare Board and others] Date Order with the signature(s) of the Judge(s) For hearing of CMA No.2421/2025 (Contempt Appln). 06.05.2026 Mr. Ghulam Ali Bozdar, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Shahryar I Awan, Assistant Advocate General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.— On 19.09.2024, the instant petition was disposed of with directions to the Selection Committee of the Sindh Workers Welfare Board to consider the case of the petitioner in light of the order passed in C.P. No. D-165 of 2019, wherein it was directed that the cases of the petitioners be considered and, if found eligible and meeting the prescribed criteria, their services be regularized. Subsequently, the petitioner, alleging non-compliance with the aforesaid directions, filed the present contempt application bearing No. 2421 of 2025. In response, Mr. Abdul Naeem Pirzada, Advocate, holding brief for the learned counsel for the respondent—Sindh Workers Welfare Board, has placed on record a statement along with the minutes of the meeting of the Selection Committee convened in compliance with the order of this Court. It has been stated therein that, during the interview/test process, the petitioner failed to demonstrate the requisite eligibility, competence, and suitability for the post and did not 2 provide satisfactory responses to the questions posed before the Selection Committee. Learned Assistant Advocate General submits that the directions of this Court have been duly complied with, as the case of the petitioner was decided by the competent forum and decided on merits.

It has been stated therein that, during the interview/test process, the petitioner failed to demonstrate the requisite eligibility, competence, and suitability for the post and did not 2 provide satisfactory responses to the questions posed before the Selection Committee. Learned Assistant Advocate General submits that the directions of this Court have been duly complied with, as the case of the petitioner was decided by the competent forum and decided on merits. Learned counsel for the petitioner, upon instructions, submits that he does not press the instant contempt application and intends to avail the appropriate remedy, if so advised, against the observations/findings recorded by the Selection Committee. In view of the above, and the compliance reported by the respondents, the instant Miscellaneous Application No. 2421 of 2025 is dismissed as not pressed. Order accordingly. J U D G E J U D G E
